title: JSON integers above 2^53 lose precision when parsed as float64 doubles
rule: Many JSON parsers decode every number into an IEEE-754 double (notably JS JSON.parse, and Go encoding/json into interface{}), so an integer above 2^53-1 silently rounds: 9007199254740993 can read back as 9007199254740992. Carry large or 64-bit ids as JSON strings, or use a parser with big-integer support.
triggers: a large integer id changes value after a JSON round-trip
JSON.parse loses precision on big integers
64-bit id read back wrong from JSON
integer above 2^53 is rounded when parsing JSON
snowflake id corrupted by JSON encoding
